Variable definition
                                             Explained variable. Reference to [52], Model (1) was constructed and calculated. The
                                           positive residual was used to measure overinvestment (OINV), and the negative residual was

PLOS ONE | https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0318328 February 7, 2025                                                                  6 / 24
 PLOS ONE                                                                                              Social credit system construction and overinvestment

used to measure underinvestment. This study retains the samples with positive residuals (mul-
                                           tiply the value by 100). The smaller the residual value, the higher the investment efficiency.
                                               INVit ¼ d0 þ d1 INVit 1 þ d2 GROWTHit 1 þ d3 LEVit 1 þ d4 CASHit 1 þ d5 AGEit 1 þ d6 SIZEit 1
                                                                                                                                                           ð1Þ
                                                       þ d7 RETURNit 1 þ YEARt þ INDi þ εit

Where INV is enterprises’ investment expenditure, GROWTH is growth level, LEV is asset-
                                           liability ratio, CASH is cash holding, AGE is listing years of enterprises, SIZE is enterprise size,
                                           RETURN is stock return rate, YEAR and IND represent the year and industry dummy variable.
                                           To make the regression results more visually presented on an appropriate scale, we expand the
                                           data on overinvestment by 100 times.
